title: Tommy Thompson Park
location: Toronto's Urban Wilderness Toggle navigation Tommy Thompson Park
description: ­ Tommy Thompson Park | Leslie Street Spit – Toronto's Urban Wilderness Toggle navigation Tommy Thompson Park | Leslie Street Spit About Join Our Direct Mailing List Visit Visitor Experience Plan Events Education Camps Bird Research Resources Donate Tommy Thompson Park Tommy Thompson Park TORONTO'S URBAN WILDERNESS PARK NOTIFICATIONS NOTICE: As part of the Invasive Species Strategy, Toronto and Region Conservation Authority (TRCA) has inventoried and continued management of Black Locust at Tommy Thompson Park. The invasion of Black Locust is rapidly expanding and outcompeting native trees and shrubs throughout the park. In fall 2025, TRCA staff treated 1 ha of Black Locust at Triangle Pond and Cell 1 using a basal bark application technique of Garlon RTU. This technique is a targeted approach where the herbicide is sprayed on the bottom portion of the tree trunk and absorbed into the roots. The treated trees will be cut and chipped in November/December 2025. The treatment areas will be monitored and re-treated as required in 2026 and will be planted with native trees and shrubs in 2027 to restore habitat for wildlife and biodiversity. description: Upcoming Events Easter Weekend 2026 – Springtime at the Conservatory! OPEN Good Friday (9am – 7pm), Saturday, Sunday & Easter Monday (10am – 5pm)! Celebrate Easter Weekend at Cambridge Butterfly Conservatory. Visit 6 stations throughout the facility to learn about our animals eggs. Then match each egg to its animal and search for the three hidden Golden Mystery Eggs to solve the final riddle. LEARN MORE March Break 2026 – Magical Morphos Visit our tropical oasis during March Break and experience Magical Morphos! Be dazzled by 1,000s of brilliant Blue Morphos in flight throughout our tropical greenhouse and take part in a fun self-guided scavenger hunt included with admission. Monarch Tagging Weekend 2026 Join us for a special weekend celebrating Monarchs and learn about their migratory journey to Mexico! description: Anniversary Summit Ontario Nature Annual Gathering 2024 © Jenna Quinn About Ontario Nature’s next Annual Gathering will be held in Kingston on Saturday, June 13, 2026. The day’s activities will include hands-on workshops, thought-provoking presentations, guided field trips and our 95th Annual General Meeting . We will be based out of Queen’s University’s Bioscience Complex and our field trips will take us to a variety of natural spaces in the beautiful Kingston area. Further program and registration details will be shared on this page in early March 2026. Please note that you must be a current member of Ontario Nature to register for this event. 85th Annual Gathering, Annual General Meeting © Michael Wynia Keep Informed Keep an eye on our events calendar and subscribe to our emails to stay informed of this and other upcoming events. Electric Vehicle Webinar 2024 June 28, 2024 12:00PM - 1:00PM Electric Car Charging Station Electric Car Charging Station 1/1 Fully Remote (Zoom) Event Details Electric vehicles have an electric motor instead of a traditional internal combustion engine. The motor runs by drawing electricity from a battery that can be charged from an external source. Additionally, electric vehicles often feature braking systems that capture and use lost energy to recharge the battery. Switching to electric vehicles is one of the best strategies to stop global warming. As of 2022, fossil fuel vehicles account for about 10% of the world's greenhouse gas emissions. But if these vehicles become electric, we could cut almost 20% of global emissions. Furthermore, EV vehicles have numerous advantages, such as cleaner air and quieter cities. Volunteer cleanups are crucial as they help protect the environment and provide clean spaces that benefit both people and aquatic life, particularly given the park's popularity for fishing. description: 2026-04-11 WELO KBA Survey WELO KBA Survey April 11 WELO KBA Survey The West End of Lake Ontario (WELO) KBA Survey takes place on the second Saturday each month from November through April. This count has 17 areas ranging from West of Dixie Rd, Mississauga to Western Niagara-on-the-Lake. To participate or find out more about the WELO KBA Survey, contact Chris Motherwell at cmtrain@cmotherwell.com for more information.

title: Bird Study Group: Looking at Our Common Birds with Wonder
description: 7:30 pm - 9:00 pm Bird Study Group: Looking at Our Common Birds with Wonder April 20 @ 7:30 pm - 9:00 pm Bird Study Group: Looking at Our Common Birds with Wonder Common birds are often overlooked in favour of the rare or more exciting species. Come and (re)discover why common birds deserve our respect (and awe!) and how they teach us to feel more present and connected to our local environment. Featured rockstar species include the Rock Pigeon, Northern Cardinal, White-breasted Nuthatch, House Sparrow and many […]

title: Browse our programs
location: Toronto (with an M postal code). Our programs are divided into 3 categories: drop-in ,
description: Browse our programs Browse our programs We have a variety of options to help you on your mental health journey. In some cases, intake is required. Simply put, intake is a session by appointment with a Stella’s Place Intake Social Worker to gather some information, understand your goals, and determine a support plan. At Stella’s Place, we provide free mental health services for young adults aged 16 to 29 and living in Toronto (with an M postal code). Our programs are divided into 3 categories: drop-in , by appointment and registered . For appointments and registered programs, you may be added to a waitlist. BeanBagChat Get one-on-one support online. Click the chat icon in the lower right corner on any page on www.stellasplace.ca to start chatting with a counsellor. How to Get Connected Individual Intake Discuss what programs you are interested in with Access Coordinators and get placed on a waitlist or signed up for a drop-in program. description: Maple Youth Wellness Hub Welcome to the Maple Youth Wellness Hub! We are a safe, welcoming space offering Integrated Youth Services (IYS) for young people all across York Region aged 12 to 25 and their families looking for mental health care, support with substance use concerns, and more. These services are free of charge, available on walk-in or appointment, and no health card is required. The Hub is located at 9401 Jane Street, Unit 321, Third Floor Maple, On. We are located right across Maple’s Auto mall and Canada’s Wonderland. In Crisis? Click Here Get to know the Maple hub Community-Based & Accessible: Located in Vaughan, the hub serves a diverse youth population with easy access to services. The space is a welcoming, youth-friendly environment, co-design with youth and community partners Integrated Youth Services Model: Youth can access mental health, substance use, primary care, peer support, and social services all in one place. title: 2025 Green Hearts Awardee – Krissan Veerasingam Apr 2, 2025 "This year GNN is thrilled to celebrate Krissan Veerasingam, a lifelong Scarborough resident, community activist, Direct
location: Scarborough resident, community activist, Director & Co-founder of Scarborough Environmenta
description: 2025 Green Hearts Awardee – Krissan Veerasingam Apr 2, 2025 "This year GNN is thrilled to celebrate Krissan Veerasingam, a lifelong Scarborough resident, community activist, Director & Co-founder of Scarborough Environmental Association (SEA), and Board Member of TTC riders. ... GNN’s first contact with Krissan was in the “tell us more” box when he signed up to join in 2022. He said he and 5 others wanted to start a new Scarborough based group to push Scarborough councillors to prioritize equitable environmental policy. And so, his journey as the co-founder of SEA began."

location: Toronto Podcast: A Mover and Shaker in Scarborough Jun 30, 2024 You need a good pair of
description: Hidden Gems Toronto Podcast: A Mover and Shaker in Scarborough Jun 30, 2024 You need a good pair of running shoes to keep up with Krissan Veerasingam. He helped launch the Scarborough Repair Café where residents can bring a multitude of household items for repair, free of charge. He is also an advocate for public transit and supports numerous environmental initiatives. As if this wasn't enough, he finds time to promote ethnic grocery stores and restaurants throughout Scarborough. And he does all this will great enthusiasm and aplomb. Residents are fortunate to have him in their corner.

location: Scarborough residents has become an unofficial tourism board Feb 3, 2024 "If you think of w
description: How a group of young, food-obsessed Scarborough residents has become an unofficial tourism board Feb 3, 2024 "If you think of what ties a neighbourhood together, it's places where people go," says Veerasingam, who led a food tour last summer highlighting places to eat along the now-shuttered Scarborough RT line. "You’ll see familiar faces week after week in places where you build connections with other customers and the people who work there."

title: Stuck on the bus: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 Residents and transit advocates see the lack of busway funding as the latest
location: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 R
description: Stuck on the bus: Toronto's 2024 budget offers no relief for Scarborough’s weary commuters Jan 13, 2024 Residents and transit advocates see the lack of busway funding as the latest example of how the area has been neglected by politicians at city hall and Queen’s Park. Successive administrations have spent decades playing political ping-pong with the replacement for the aging RT — at one point it was going to be an LRT — ultimately leaving Scarborough residents today without rapid transit for years. “Sometimes it feels like the city views Scarborough residents’ time as less valuable," said Krissan Veerasingam, board member for transit advocacy group TTCriders.

location: Toronto organized Repair Café this past year. In particular, Scarborough Environment As
description: Repair Café takes big strides with our new leaders! Dec 31, 2023 A good number of community organizations in Toronto organized Repair Café this past year. In particular, Scarborough Environment Association (SEA) began to offer Repair Café monthly. Krissan and Fizza, at SEA, share about their new Repair Café startup and what Repair Café means to them.

location: Toronto’s transportation system.
description: Fizza Khalid Speaks at Moving Together Conference Nov 14, 2023 Environmental Defence hosted Moving Together: Creating the Transportation Future We Want, an event moderated by Uytae Lee of CBC’s Stories About Here. The gathering brought together community experts like Fizza Khalid and engaged the public in discussions on building a clean and equitable transportation future. Mayor Olivia Chow attended and shared her vision for the future of Toronto’s transportation system.

location: Scarborough RT remains out of service following derailment Aug 16, 2023 "I think they're ma
description: TTC looking at options as Scarborough RT remains out of service following derailment Aug 16, 2023 "I think they're making the best of a bad situation, but they could make better of a bad situation and that's why I advocate for the rapid lanes, and of course the busway," said Krissan Veerasingam with TTC Riders. "We need to see these solutions like bus lanes and, more importantly, a busway because, of course, you can't fit 70 buses an hour realistically on a Scarborough street."

title: Tenants protest province's Bill 23 Nov 23, 2022 Fizza Khalid of the Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through increasing housing de
location: Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through
description: Tenants protest province's Bill 23 Nov 23, 2022 Fizza Khalid of the Scarborough Environmental Association said the only way to help the environment is through increasing housing density in cities, but she said she believes the Progressive Conservative government and developers want to build more suburban sprawl in Ontario’s Greenbelt instead. “I have a full-time job; I went to university. I can’t even afford a place to live,” Khalid said. “You expect me to move out to the Greenbelt.”

description: Enjoy and protect nature with us Toronto Field Naturalists Enjoy and protect nature with us! Founded in 1923, the Toronto Field Naturalists (TFN) is a volunteer-run non-profit nature conservation organization. We connect people with nature in the Toronto area, helping them to understand, enjoy, and protect Toronto’s green spaces and the species that inhabit them. Learn more about the benefits to membership to decide if Toronto Field Naturalists is right for you. And don’t forget to check out our events calendar ! description: Lucky Lion Night Market June 12 – 14 + June 12 – 14 Lucky Lion Night Market Step into the vibrant energy of an Asian night market with Lucky Lion Night Market! 📅 June 12 to 14, 2025 🕛 FRI: 4 PM – Midnight | SAT: 2 PM – Midnight | SUN: 12 PM – 8 PM 🎟️ FREE ENTRY What To Expect: 🍜 Bold and flavourful street eats 🦁 Traditional Lion Dance performances 🎶 Live entertainment 🛍️ Curated artisan market 🎯 Interactive games & prize booths 📸 Picture-perfect photo ops and displays 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family-friendly atmosphere Experience the buzz of late-night bites, bright lights, and cultural flair. Visit www.luckylionnightmarket.ca for more information.

title: HopFest
location: Vaughan’s egg-citing celebration! Bring the whole family for a day filled with sweet tr
description: HopFest April 3 – 5 + April 3 – 5 HopFest Hop into the springtime fun at HopFest , Vaughan’s egg-citing celebration! Bring the whole family for a day filled with sweet treats, festive activities, and playful surprises around every corner. 📅 April 3 – 5, 2026 🕛 10 AM – 6 PM 🎟️ Admission & Activity Tickets Available for Purchase What to Expect: 🐰 Egg Hunts – Join the fun and find hidden treasures throughout the garden 🎨 Garden Crafts – Create colourful keepsakes to take home (Saturday only, while quantities last) 🍭 Sweet Treats & Eats – Enjoy delicious snacks and seasonal goodies 🎠 Carnival Rides & Games – Laugh, play, and make magical memories 📸 Bunny Photo Ops – Capture your hoppiest moments with the Easter Bunny 🛍️ Vendor Market — Presented by Toronto Made Market Costumes and bunny ears encouraged — smiles guaranteed! Get your tickets here! Visit www.hopfest.ca for more information.

title: Vaughan International Music Festival
location: Vaughan International Music Festival July 4 – 5 + July 4 – 5 Vaughan International Musi
description: Vaughan International Music Festival July 4 – 5 + July 4 – 5 Vaughan International Music Festival VIMF 2025 is Back – Bigger and Bolder! 📅 June 28 to 29, 2025 🕛 4 PM – 11 PM (both days) 🎟️ FREE ENTRY Get ready for the 4th Annual Vaughan International Music Festival (VIMF) – York Region’s premier celebration of music, culture, and diversity! Join thousands of festival-goers for two unforgettable days of: 🎶 Live music from sensational local & international talent 💃 Cultural dance performances & workshops 🍔 Delicious global cuisine 🍷 Beer & wine garden 🎨 Family-friendly fun & activities 📢 Mark your calendars and stay tuned for artist announcements and exciting surprises! Let the rhythms of the world move you at #VIMF2025 ! Visit www.myvimf.com for more information.

title: Asado
location: Toronto Latinos , get ready for 2 days of sizzling grills, Latin beats, and unforgettab
description: Asado June 28 – 29 + June 28 – 29 Asado Canada’s biggest Asado fest is coming to Assembly Park Presented by Copa Cabana & Toronto Latinos , get ready for 2 days of sizzling grills, Latin beats, and unforgettable vibes! 📅 June 28 & 29, 2025 🕛 12 PM – 11 PM (both days) 🎟️ FREE ENTRY before 6 PM | All Ages Welcomed | Registration Required What To Expect: 🌍 A vibrant cultural marketplace with 40+ Latin vendors 🎶 Non-stop Latin music & live entertainment 🔥 Authentic Asado cuisine grilled by top chefs 🥩 Copa Cabana—one of Canada’s top steakhouses—is taking the lead on food! 🩴 The FIRST-EVER Chancla Throwing Contest 🕺 Dancing, drinks, and an electric community vibe Visit www.torontolatinos.com for your complimentary tickets and more information.

title: Vaughan Ribfest
location: Vaughan Ribfest May 16 – 19 + May 16 – 19 Vaughan Ribfest Vaughan Ribfest 2025 is back
description: Vaughan Ribfest May 16 – 19 + May 16 – 19 Vaughan Ribfest Vaughan Ribfest 2025 is back this Victoria Day Long Weekend — and it’s bringing the BBQ heat ! Presented by Ontario Festival Group, join us for four days of finger-lickin’ fun! 📅 May 16 to 19, 2025 🕛 FRI – SUN: 12 PM – 10 PM | MON: 12 PM – 7 PM 🎟️ Admission: $5 | Kids 10 & under FREE 🚗 Free Parking | 🐾 Pet Friendly | 🎤 Free Concerts with Admission What To Expect: 🥩 Smoked Ribs, Brisket & Pulled Pork 🍗 BBQ Chicken & Sausages 🦴 Massive “Dino” Beef Ribs 🍟 Festival eats like Bloomin’ Onions, Poutines, Roasted Corn & more 🍻 Ice-cold beer to top it off! 🎶 Live Entertainment 🎡 Plus: Midway rides & carnival games for the whole fam! Come hungry. Leave happy. #VaughanRibfest2025 Visit www.ribfestx.com/event/vaughan-ribfest-2025/ for tickets and more information.

title: Vaughan International Music Festival
location: Vaughan International Music Festival August 23 – 24 + August 23 – 24 Vaughan Internatio
description: Vaughan International Music Festival August 23 – 24 + August 23 – 24 Vaughan International Music Festival The VIMF, Established As The 1st International Music Festival In The City Of Vaughan And York Region, Celebrates Diversity, Culture And Inclusiveness Through Music. The Festival Showcases A Wide Range Of Musical Genres, Including Pop, Hip-Hop, Rock, Classical, Jazz And World Music, And Features Performances By Both Established And Emerging Artists. A Key Objective Is To Support Young, Local, Aspiring Artists By Providing A Platform To Showcase Talent And Gain Exposure. The VIMF Has Created A Scholarship Program To Help Provide Funding Support To Young Artists. The VIMF Aims To Promote Cultural Exchange And Understanding By Bringing Together Musicians And Audiences From Different Backgrounds And Communities. The Festival Continues To Be A Major Tourism Attraction That Draws Visitors From Across The Region And Beyond, Providing Significant Economic Boosts To Vaughan’s Downtown And Lo

title: Dance Workshops
location: vaughan to book your free class.
description: Dance Workshops July 6 + July 6 Dance Workshops Bollywood 101 Workshop Saturday, July 6 · 12 – 1pm Join Karishma Mansingani in this super fun workshop! Bollywood dancing comes from the Indian film industry and incorporates traditional Indian and Bhangra folk dance with Hip Hop, Jazz, Latin and Bellydance styles. It is energetic, expressive and so much fun! No prior dance experience necessary, pre-registration required. Bring the whole family! Line Dance 101 Workshop Saturday, July 6 · 1 – 2pm Join Rhea Daniels and learn basic steps and dance sequences to Pop, R&B, and Country music that you can take to the next wedding, a barbecue or a house party. Line dancing is a fun and social way to stay active. No prior dance experience is necessary. Online registration required (one per person). Pricing includes HST. location: Vaughan International Film Festival June 17 – 19 + June 17 – 19 Vaughan International F
description: Vaughan International Film Festival June 17 – 19 + June 17 – 19 Vaughan International Film Festival The 11th Vaughan International Film Festival (VFF) launches its 2024 edition with the Student Screening at Assembly Park on June 17, showcasing 18 short films created by high school students from across Ontario. During the event, three finalists will be announced and awarded Lumix camera equipment, followed by a networking session with industry professionals. As a not-for-profit organization near Toronto, VFF is dedicated to advancing the short film industry through screenings, scholarships, and educational events. The festival serves as a vital platform for emerging and established filmmakers, drawing an audience of over 20,000 attendees each year. The full lineup can be found at vaughanfilmfestival.com and tickets can be purchased here .

title: Food Truck’N Fridays

description: Food Truck’N Fridays June 14 – September 27 + June 14 – September 27 Food Truck’N Fridays Food Truck’N Friday is a once a week celebration every Friday from 5-8 p.m. Food Truck’N Friday has found a new home for the summer. The hugely popular food truck event series, which ran weekly from 2015 to 2022 in the GTA is back this summer at Assembly Park. The expansive public space, located close to countless retail and transit connections, will feature a daily assembly of Toronto’s best food trucks from June 7 to Sept. 27, 2024. As part of this summer series, Food Truck’N Friday will be bringing its unique concept that combines food truck eats, artisans and entertainment to the park on every Friday. “Food Truck’N Friday at Assembly Park is tapped to become a hot new summer destination for the Greater Toronto Area,” says Jason Bellissimo, Founder of Food Truck’N Friday and Principal at J.B. Innovative Solutions Inc. “You’ll find all the things you loved about Food Truck’N Friday in Etobicoke

description: SUMMER DANCE ASSEMBLY with the Dance Together Project June 8 + June 8 SUMMER DANCE ASSEMBLY with the Dance Together Project To celebrate a fantastic first season of Dance Together Residencies and community dance workshop programming in partnership with Assembly Park and York Region Arts Council , we welcome you at the Summer Dance Assembly on June 8th, 2024. Join us for a special event, co-curated by dance artists Katya Kuznetsova and Mellisa Hart . title: Vaughan Ribfest
location: Vaughan Ribfest May 17 – 20 + May 17 – 20 Vaughan Ribfest The 2nd Annual Vaughan Ribfes
description: Vaughan Ribfest May 17 – 20 + May 17 – 20 Vaughan Ribfest The 2nd Annual Vaughan Ribfest will take place at the outdoor event grounds at Assembly Park Vaughan convenient for all surrounding communities. Produced by Ontario Festival Group we are bringing 5 of North America’s Best Rib Teams coming from Daytona Beach, Florida, Brewton, Alabama, Castleberry, Alabama, Fort Erie, Ontario and London, Ontario. These “Pitmasters” are ready to serve you and your friends Award-Winning Mouth-Watering BBQ Smoked Ribs. There will be a variety of festival foods like mini-donuts, roasted corn, poutines, funnel cakes and of course to wash it all down nice cold beer. There will be plenty of activities including a Spectacular Midway, Carnival Games, Marketplace, Artisans, Rib Eating Contest and more… Watch Live Music all weekend long featuring amazing Tributes to Bob Marley, ACDC The Hip plus local bands. Follow Us @VaughanRibfest Vaughan Ribfest

description: Dance Together Open Studio January 27 + January 27 Dance Together Open Studio As part of Dance Together’s new 2024 season, they will be hosting several Open Studio events at Assembly Park. title: Mashup Pon di Road
description: Mashup Pon di Road July 19 + July 19 Mashup Pon di Road MASHUP PON DI ROAD is a bashment circus on a big truck—an outdoor, vaudeville-style, West-Indian flavoured, black girl-powered variety show pulling up on city streets. Fusing raucous humour with whip-smart cultural critique, MASHUP PON DI ROAD pokes fun at the constraints of the patriarchy, the expectations of womanhood, and the end of the world as we know it. through a series of vignettes and songs, two ringmasters and their band of colourful misfits rum punch up at the ruling class, giving you a funhouse mirror look at the sheer absurdity of dis oppressive world. the path to liberation sparkles with laughter. let’s get unruly. Touching road summer 2022. Tickets are free – register your spot with the link below – come one, come all! Welcome to di circus! Community Preview: July 19, 2022 , gates at 8 p.m. / show at 9 p.m. @ Assembly Park – 80 Interchange Way – Sponsored by QuadReal Property Group REGISTER YOUR SPOT HERE
